{{Infobox location
| name = Cana
| introduced = tg-02-crimson-vault
}}

''See also: [[Cana Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Cana serves as the capital city of [[Damasca]] and is a significant location within the realm. It is governed by an [[Overlord]] and contains various landmarks, including a [[Grandmasters' palace]], massive stone walls with metal doors, and a [[Hanging Tree]]. Historically, the city has been a major hub for travelers and citizens passing through its gates. The city's landscape has faced significant destruction, with its walls reduced to ruins by the powers of different [[Incarnations]]. At various points in history, Cana has been subject to being sealed off by a crimson dome of light that prevents passage through any world, and it has also been the site of major events such as the burning of a tree. While it can be accessed via [[Gates]], these connections have been known to fail or become impassable during times of conflict.}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Cana serves as the capital city of [[Damasca]] and a significant realm that houses the Royal Palace and the [[Hanging Tree]]. Located southeast of the current battlefield, the city is under the command of [[Talos]]. Following a meeting in [[the House]], [[Denner]] and [[Indirial]] returned to Cana with [[Simon]]. Although an attack on the city was being planned by the [[Grandmasters]], who desired to see it burn, the [[Incarnation]] has since sealed Cana so that there is no way in or out.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The Royal Palace is located in [[Cana]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Cana]] is the capital city of [[Damasca]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Cana]] is a capital city.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Cana]] is a location southeast of the current battlefield.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c11">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Grandmaster Endross]] wishes to see [[Cana]] burn.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Cana]] is a realm.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Grandmasters]] intend to strike at [[Cana]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Cana]] is a location that the [[Damascan army]] might strike.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c16">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|An attack on [[Cana]] is being planned.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Denner]] and [[Indirial]] met [[Simon]] in [[the House]] and returned with him to [[Cana]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c17">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Hanging Tree]] is located in [[Cana]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c19">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Cana]] is a location containing a royal palace.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Cana]] is a city containing a [[Tree]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|There is no way in or out of [[Cana]] because the [[Incarnation]] has it sealed up tight.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c25">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Cana]] is a location commanded by [[Talos]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c25" />}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Cana serves as the [[Damascan]] capital, a city characterized by its stone walls and massive metal doors set into stone. For six months, the location was sealed beneath a crimson dome of light that turned its sky scarlet, and during this time, the [[Overlord of Cana]] borrowed personnel from Feiora. While [[Rhalia]] and [[Cynara]] engaged in combat within the streets, rumors suggested that [[Incarnations]] might be gathering there. Although the city can be reached via a Gate, various groups have avoided the area since its barrier fell. Currently, [[King]] [[Zakareth]] stands upon the city's walls, claiming to be securing the site despite the fact that the top of the fortifications lies in ruins.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Cana]] is a city.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2">''City of Light'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Cana]] is the [[Damascan]] capital and has been sealed within a barrier of crimson power for six months.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c4">''City of Light'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Overlord of Cana]] borrowed some of Feiora's personnel.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7">''City of Light'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The city of [[Cana]] is covered by a dome of red light.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9">''City of Light'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Rhalia]] and [[Cynara]] fought in the streets of [[Cana]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Incarnations]] might be gathering in [[Cana]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Cana]]'s sky is crimson.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11">''City of Light'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Cana]] has a scarlet dome in its sky.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Cana]] has gates and massive metal doors set into stone.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c15">''City of Light'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Cana]] is a location that can be reached via a Gate.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17">''City of Light'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Cana]] is a location ruled by an [[Overlord]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Cana]] is a city that can be sealed.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]] would have headed to [[Cana]] upon learning [[Indirial]] was captured.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19">''City of Light'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The camp has been avoiding [[Cana]] since its barrier went down.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] stands on the walls of [[Cana]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Kai]] walked onto the walls of [[Cana]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22">''City of Light'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] claims to have been securing the city of [[Cana]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The stone walls of [[Cana]] are visible miles away from the tent.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Cana]] has walls.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c23">''City of Light'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The top of [[Cana]]'s wall is in ruins.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c24">''City of Light'', chapter 24</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“We would see Cana burn,” Grandmaster Endross said.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|“I have been away for some time, securing the city of Cana.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Locations]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate locations]]
[[Category:Settlements]]
